title: [Beedrill] Battle Guide

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Beedrill] is a lightweight [Bug]/[Poison] physical [pivot] whose Champions role revolves entirely around [u-turn] tempo and [knock-off] utility. 90 Attack with 75 Speed looks modest on paper, but pivoting safely, stripping items, and punishing Fairies and Grasses from one slot makes it a glue piece for [pivot]-heavy cores.

## Strengths
[u-turn] is the whole identity — every successful hit gives the team a free matchup re-evaluation and deals damage on the way out. [poison-jab] breaks Fairies and mirror [Grass], [drill-run] punishes Steels and Rocks that try to wall the [Bug] STAB, and [knock-off] eats the opposing item economy. Across a long match, that item removal alone can decide games.

## Weaknesses & Answers
40 Defense is extremely fragile — any physical neutral hit threatens a KO, and the [Rock], [Fire], [Flying], and [psychic] weaknesses are all common coverage moves. Priority attackers push [Beedrill] out of the game without [focus-sash], so item choice is non-optional. [swarm] only activates below 1/3 HP, so the damage floor is low against bulky walls; you have to force trades by pivoting into soft matchups.

## Recommended Moveset
[u-turn] is locked as the main [Bug] STAB because [pivot] tempo is the whole set. [poison-jab] is the mandatory [Poison] STAB for Fairies, and [drill-run] is the [Ground] coverage that punishes Steels, Rocks, and Electrics trying to wall the combo. The last slot is [knock-off] for item removal and long-game resource generation. A variant that replaces [knock-off] with [swords-dance] converts [Beedrill] into a one-shot setup sweeper.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] is the default because [Beedrill] needs one guaranteed survival turn against priority. [choice-scarf] is the alternative when the team needs a [revenge] killer, trading flexibility for Speed. [black-glasses] boosts [knock-off] into a harder hit, making the utility move double as a damage spike. Keep [swarm] as the ability for the low-HP damage spike. Pick Tera [Bug] for STAB damage or Tera [Steel] for the [Rock], [Flying], and [psychic] patch.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Beedrill] slots into [pivot] cores alongside [volt-switch] and [flip-turn] users to keep team momentum spinning. Pair it with a physical wall to cover the 40 Defense hole and a dedicated hazard remover so it can enter the field repeatedly without chip. A setup sweeper behind the [pivot] cycle capitalises on the chip and item removal it generates.

## Play Patterns
Lead with [Beedrill] into a predicted passive matchup to start the [u-turn] loop, or bring it in on turn two to force a [knock-off] trade. Use Tera on a telegraphed [Flying] or [Rock] attack, and only hold position when [swarm] is active and a guaranteed KO is on the table.
